name: pyflutterflow

metadata.name: pyflutterflow
metadata.summary: The companion python library to Flutterflow

documentation:
# Pyflutterflow

A python companion to the flutterflow ecosystem.

PyFlutterflow is a companion to flutterflow, allowing CRUD operations in firestore and also MongoDB,
handling advanced push notification handling features, and providing an admin dashboard to handle
it all.

classes.name: pyflutterflow.database.firestore.firestore_client.FirestoreClient

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.firestore.firestore_client.FirestoreClient.set_client
documentation:
Sets the singleton Firestore AsyncClient instance.

Args:
    client (AsyncClient): An instance of Firestore AsyncClient to be used as the singleton.

Raises:
    ValueError: If an attempt is made to set the client when it's already initialized.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.firestore.firestore_client.FirestoreClient.get_client
documentation:
Retrieves the singleton Firestore AsyncClient instance.

Returns:
    AsyncClient: The initialized Firestore AsyncClient instance.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the Firestore client has not been initialized.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.firestore.firestore_client.FirestoreClient.close_client
documentation:
Closes the singleton Firestore AsyncClient instance.

This method gracefully closes the Firestore client, ensuring that all pending
operations are completed and resources are released. After closing, the client
instance is set to `None`, allowing for re-initialization if necessary.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the Firestore client has not been initialized.
    Exception: Propagates any exceptions raised during the closing of the client.

Singleton class to manage a single instance of the Firestore AsyncClient.

This class ensures that only one instance of the Firestore client exists throughout
the application's lifecycle. It provides class methods to set, retrieve, and close
the Firestore client, facilitating centralized management of Firestore interactions.

Attributes:
    _client (Optional[AsyncClient]): The singleton instance of the Firestore AsyncClient.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.database.mongodb.mongo_client.initialize_mongodb
documentation:
Initialize the MongoDB connection and Beanie ODM with defined document models.

Connects to the MongoDB database using credentials from settings and initializes
Beanie with the specified document models for ORM functionality.

classes.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_client.SupabaseClient

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_client.SupabaseClient.__init__
documentation: Initialize environment settings and placeholders for Supabase client.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_client.SupabaseClient.initialize_client
documentation: Initializes the Supabase Client instance asynchronously.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_client.SupabaseClient.get_client
documentation:
Retrieves the Supabase Client instance.

Returns:
    AsyncClient: The initialized Supabase Client instance.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the Supabase client has not been initialized.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_client.SupabaseClient.close_client
documentation:
Closes the Supabase Client instance.

This method gracefully resets the Supabase client, allowing for re-initialization if necessary.

Singleton class to manage a single instance of the Supabase Client.

This class ensures that only one instance of the Supabase client exists throughout
the application's lifecycle. It provides methods to initialize, retrieve, and close
the Supabase client, facilitating centralized management of Supabase interactions.

classes.name: pyflutterflow.database.mongodb.mongo_repository.MongoRepository
documentation:
This is a base service class that provides CRUD operations for whatever model inherits from it.
It interacts with the model layer and raises http exceptions where necessary. Some methods,
such as get_by_id() and delete(), can be used directly. Others will need to be called from a
more specific service class that inherits from this one, since input data types will need to be
specified.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_functions.generate_jwt
documentation:
Generates a JWT token for Supabase authentication.

Args:
    user_id (str): The ID of the user for whom the token is generated.
    is_admin (bool, optional): Flag indicating if the user has admin privileges. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    str: A signed JWT token for authenticating with Supabase.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_functions.get_token
documentation:
Retrieves or generates a JWT token for the specified user, caching the result for efficiency.

Args:
    user_id (str): The ID of the user for whom the token is generated or retrieved.
    role (str, optional): The role of the user, used to determine if admin privileges are required. Defaults to ''.

Returns:
    dict: A dictionary containing the 'Authorization' header with the Bearer token.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_functions.supabase_request
documentation:
Forwards an HTTP request to Supabase with appropriate authentication headers.

Args:
    request (Request): The incoming FastAPI request.
    path (str): The Supabase endpoint path to which the request is forwarded.
    current_user (FirebaseUser, optional): The current authenticated user, injected by dependency.

Returns:
    Response: A FastAPI Response object containing the Supabase response data.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_functions.proxy
documentation:
Proxy function to handle forwarding a request to Supabase.

Args:
    request (Request): The incoming FastAPI request.
    path (str): The Supabase endpoint path to which the request is forwarded.
    current_user (FirebaseUser, optional): The current authenticated user, injected by dependency.

Returns:
    Response: A FastAPI Response object containing the Supabase response data.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_functions.proxy_with_body
documentation:
Proxy function for forwarding a request with a body payload to Supabase.

Args:
    request (Request): The incoming FastAPI request.
    body (dict): The request body data to forward.
    path (str): The Supabase endpoint path to which the request is forwarded.
    current_user (FirebaseUser, optional): The current authenticated user, injected by dependency.

Returns:
    Response: A FastAPI Response object containing the Supabase response data.

classes.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_repository.SupabaseRepository

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_repository.SupabaseRepository.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the repository with the given model.

Args:
    model (type[ModelType]): The model class to use for database operations.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the model does not have a Settings class with a 'name' attribute.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_repository.SupabaseRepository.paginator
documentation:
Calculates the start and end indices for pagination based on the given parameters.

Args:
    params (Params): The pagination parameters.

Returns:
    Tuple[int, int]: The start and end indices for the pagination.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_repository.SupabaseRepository.build_paginated_query
documentation:
Builds a paginated query for fetching records, optionally with authentication headers.

Args:
    params (Params): The pagination parameters.
    current_user (FirebaseUser): The currently authenticated user.
    sql_query (str): The SQL query string for selecting fields.
    auth (bool): Whether to include authentication headers in the query.

Returns:
    Any: The constructed query object ready for execution.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_repository.SupabaseRepository.build_query
documentation:
Builds a query for fetching records from the specified table, optionally with authentication headers.

Args:
    current_user (FirebaseUser): The currently authenticated user.
    sql_query (str, optional): The SQL query string for selecting fields. Defaults to '*'.
    is_delete_query (bool, optional): Whether the query is for deleting records. Defaults to False.
    auth (bool, optional): Whether to include authentication headers in the query. Defaults to True.
    table (str, optional): The name of the table to query. Defaults to None, which uses the model's table name.

Returns:
    Any: The constructed query object ready for execution.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_repository.SupabaseRepository.list_all
documentation:
Retrieves a paginated and optionally sorted list of all records.

Args:
    params (Params): The pagination parameters.
    current_user (FirebaseUser): The currently authenticated user.
    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments.
        - sql_query (str, optional): The SQL query string for selecting fields. Defaults to '*'.
        - auth (bool, optional): Whether to include authentication headers in the query. Defaults to True.
        - sort_by (str, optional): The field name to sort the records by.

Returns:
    Page[ModelType]: A paginated list of records.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_repository.SupabaseRepository.get
documentation:
Retrieves a single record by primary key (ID), ensuring it belongs to the current user.

Args:
    pk (int): The primary key (ID) of the record to retrieve.
    current_user (FirebaseUser): The currently authenticated user.
    auth (bool, optional): Whether to include authentication headers in the query. Defaults to True.

Returns:
    ModelType: The retrieved record.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the record does not exist or the operation fails.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_repository.SupabaseRepository.create
documentation:
Creates a new record with the current user's ID.

Args:
    data (CreateSchemaType): The data to create the record.
    current_user (FirebaseUser): The currently authenticated user.

Returns:
    ModelType: The created record.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the create operation fails.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_repository.SupabaseRepository.create_multiple
documentation:
Creates N new records.

Args:
    data (CreateSchemaType): The data to create the record.

Returns:
    ModelType: The created record.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the create operation fails.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_repository.SupabaseRepository.update
documentation:
Updates an existing record by primary key (ID), ensuring it belongs to the current user.

Args:
    pk (int): The primary key (ID) of the record to update.
    data (UpdateSchemaType): The data to update the record with.
    current_user (FirebaseUser): The currently authenticated user.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the update operation fails.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_repository.SupabaseRepository.delete
documentation:
Deletes a record by primary key (ID). Note this is an admin
operation and does not check if the record belongs to the current user.
Use
    await query.delete().eq("id", pk).eq("user_id", current_user.uid).execute()
explicitly to check for ownership.

Args:
    pk (int): The primary key (ID) of the record to delete.
    current_user (FirebaseUser): The currently authenticated user.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the delete operation fails.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.database.supabase.supabase_repository.SupabaseRepository.restricted_delete
documentation: Deletes a record by primary key (ID), ensuring it belongs to the current user.
A repository class for interacting with Supabase, providing CRUD operations for a given model.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.services.notifications.fcm.PushNotificationService.__init__
documentation: Initialize the PushNotificationService with a Firestore client instance.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.services.notifications.fcm.PushNotificationService.get_user_fcm_tokens
documentation:
Retrieve the FCM tokens associated with a specific user.

Args:
    user_id (str): The ID of the user whose FCM tokens are to be fetched.

Returns:
    list[str]: A list of FCM token strings for the user.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.services.notifications.fcm.PushNotificationService.get_all_users
documentation:
Retrieve all user IDs from the Firestore database.

Returns:
    list[str]: A list of user IDs.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.services.notifications.fcm.PushNotificationService.send_notification_to_users
documentation:
Send a notification to multiple users by their user IDs.

Args:
    user_ids (list[str]): A list of user IDs to whom the notification will be sent.
    notification (Notification): The notification content to be sent.
    badge (int, optional): The badge count for the notification on iOS devices. Defaults to 1.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.services.notifications.fcm.PushNotificationService.send_notification_to_devices
documentation:
Create a notification message and send it to multiple devices based on their FCM tokens.

Args:
    user_tokens (list[str]): A list of FCM tokens representing target devices.
    notification (Notification): The notification content to be sent.
    badge (int, optional): The badge count for the notification on iOS devices. Defaults to 1.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.services.notifications.fcm.PushNotificationService.add_user_to_topic
documentation:
Subscribe a user to a specific topic for future notifications.

Args:
    user_id (str): The ID of the user to subscribe.
    topic (str): The topic to which the user will be subscribed.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.services.notifications.fcm.PushNotificationService.remove_user_from_topic
documentation:
Unsubscribe a user from a specific topic.

Args:
    user_id (str): The ID of the user to unsubscribe.
    topic (str): The topic from which the user will be unsubscribed.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.services.notifications.fcm.PushNotificationService.send_notification_to_topic
documentation:
Send a notification message to all devices subscribed to a specific topic.

Args:
    topic (str): The topic to which the notification will be sent.
    notification (Notification): The notification content to be sent.
    routing_data (dict): Additional data for routing or handling the notification.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.auth.get_admin_user
documentation: Verify the JWT token, check for the admin service role, and then return the user object.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.auth.get_current_user
documentation: Verify the JWT token and return the user object.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.auth.get_users_list
documentation: Get a list of all users in the firebase auth system.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.auth.get_firebase_user_by_uid
documentation: Get a list of all users in the firebase auth system.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.auth.run_supabase_firestore_user_sync
documentation:
Run a sync of Firebase users with Supabase users. Note this function
would need to be upgraded to handle large user lists (>1000 users).

functions.name: pyflutterflow.auth.set_user_role
documentation: Update the service role permissions on the desired firebase user account. Take care: this action can create an admin.

classes.name: pyflutterflow.auth.FirebaseUser
documentation:
When a firebase auth token is validated and decoded, this
is the structure of the user data returned.

classes.name: pyflutterflow.auth.FirebaseAuthUser
documentation:
The user structure from the firebase auth Python SDK differs slightly from
FirebaseUser, so this model is used to represent that user object.

classes.name: pyflutterflow.auth.FirebaseUserClaims
documentation:
Some tokens have custom claims, and this feature is used by pyflutterflow
to assign and manage admin rights at the token level.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.routes.serve_vue_config
documentation:
This route serves configuration for the Vue.js admin dashboard.

The dashboard consumes this configuration to connect to the Firebase and Supabase APIs,
and to handle the correct database schema.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.routes.set_role
documentation:
Set a role (e.g. admin) for a firebase auth user. This will create a custom
claim in the user's token, available in all requests.

Also sets a flag called 'is_admin' in the supabase users table. If the users
table is not called 'users', please set the USERS_TABLE environment variable.
########## Firebase auth routes ##############

functions.name: pyflutterflow.routes.get_users
documentation: Get a list of all Firebase users. This route is only accessible to admins.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.routes.get_user_by_id
documentation: Get a Firebase user by their UID. This route is only accessible to admins.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.routes.supabase_firestore_user_sync
documentation:
Sync Firebase users with Supabase users. This will create a new user in the
Supabase users table for each Firebase user, if not present.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.routes.supabase_get_proxy
documentation: ########## SUPABASE PROXY ROUTES ##############
These routes are used to proxy requests to the Supabase API.
You can use them just as you would use the Supabase REST API
(i.e. the postgrest API). However, the proxy serves to overcome
one of Flutterflow's main limitations: the inability to use
Firebase auth tokens in the Supabase API. To achieve this, these
routes will mint a new supabase JWT token based on the Firebase
one, including admin privilages. Minted admin tokens will have
an embedded 'user_role'='admin' claim, which can be used in RLS
to authenticate admin requests.

functions.name: pyflutterflow.routes.cloudinary_upload
documentation:
Upload an image to Cloudinary. This will return a JSON object containing
urls for the image in common sizes, such as thumbnails and display sizes.

methods.name: pyflutterflow.BaseModels.AppBaseModel.to_dict
documentation: Use this in place of pydantic's .model_dump() to avoid issues with PydanticObjectId.

class_variables.name: pyflutterflow.paginator.Page.page
documentation: Set as optional with a default value

class_variables.name: pyflutterflow.paginator.Page.size
documentation: Set as optional with a default value

class_variables.name: pyflutterflow.paginator.Page.pages
documentation: Set as optional with a default value

functions.name: pyflutterflow.middleware.handle_uncaught_exceptions
documentation: Send a message to Slack when an uncaught exception occurs

functions.name: pyflutterflow.middleware.handle_validation_error
documentation: Exception handler to get more information about 422 errors. Not technically invoked as middleware, but acts as such.
